texto: What we actually know firsthand is minuscule: the feel of the spring air on our skin, our own private daydreams and phobias. Outside of these tiny warrens of private knowledge, we have to depend on what others say.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Christopher L. Hayes sobre temas ligados a motivacao. A afirmação “What we actually know firsthand is minuscule: the feel of the spring air on our skin, our own pri…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, information, reflexao, útil em momentos de termino e reflexao.

frase_original: What we actually know firsthand is minuscule: the feel of the spring air on our skin, our own private daydreams and phobias. Outside of these tiny warrens of private knowledge, we have to depend on what others say.

texto: Imagine a weary sailor coming home to port in the midst of a brutal storm. Along the horizon he sees the burning lights of dozens of lighthouses. And yet he knows from experience that some are so old they've receded miles inland as the shore has grown. Others are simply fakes, put out by sadists and rivals. To be a citizen in these strange times is to perpetually find oneself in that poor sailor's perilous state. We know that danger lurks in the darkness, but we don't know if we have the means to avoid it.
explicacao: Citação de Christopher L. Hayes sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Imagine a weary sailor coming home to port in the midst of a brutal storm.
